page: Amazon Web Services i4i.metal is a bare-metal, storage-optimized server instance powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The instance includes a massive local storage capacity of 30,000 GB delivered via NVMe SSDs, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights the exceptional multi-core capabilities of this bare-metal configuration. In multi-core CPU performance, the instance ranks in the top tier (top 10%) for stress-ng, Passmark CPU, and Geekbench ray tracing and image processing tests. Memory bandwidth is strong, placing in the top 25% for both cached small-block and uncached large-block read/write operations. Database operations and Redis SET operations also perform in the top 25%, demonstrating highly efficient data processing capabilities, while single-core performance remains in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i4i.metal offers high resource density by bundling massive local NVMe storage and a substantial memory footprint on dedicated bare-metal hardware. This eliminates virtualization overhead, though it represents a high-end resource commitment. The instance is highly suited for large-scale databases, transactional workloads, data warehousing, and high-throughput static web serving where local storage speed and multi-core processing are critical.
description: Amazon Web Services i4i.metal is a bare-metal, storage-optimized server designed for high-throughput workloads. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor featuring 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) running at 3.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a substantial 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth, placing in the top 10% for Passmark CPU and Geekbench ray tracing tests. Its database and Redis SET operations rank in the top 25%, reflecting its high-efficiency data handling. This bare-metal instance is optimized for large-scale databases, transactional systems, and data-intensive applications requiring direct hardware access and massive local storage capacity.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.metal is a bare-metal, storage-optimized server featuring 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, and 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 Intel vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 30,000 GB NVMe SSD storage for intensive database workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, storage-optimized instance combining massive local NVMe capacity with high-density multi-core processing for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs operating at 3.5 GHz
30,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
1024 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read/write bandwidth
Top-tier multi-core CPU and image processing benchmark performance
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Best suited for large-scale databases, transactional systems, and data warehousing
